content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all prime numbers in the range [LLL, RRR].


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains two space separated integers, LLL and RRR - The start and end of the range.


Output Format

Print on a single line, all prime numbers between LLL and RRR (inclusive) in increasing order with a space between them.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of input contains two space-separated integers, L and R, denoting the start and end of the range.
content.outputFormat: Print on a single line, all prime numbers between L and R (inclusive) in increasing order with a space between them.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The primes between 2 and 11 inclusive are 2, 3, 5, 7 and 11.
Only the numbers 11 and 13 are prime in the given range, so they are printed separated by a space.
The range consists of a single number 5, which is prime, therefore the output is just "5".

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm to generate all prime numbers up to 1000. Then, for each test case, we simply print the prime numbers within the given range.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log log N) for the Sieve of Eratosthenes, where N is 1000. Then, O(R - L) for printing the prime numbers in each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the prime numbers, where N is 1000.
